log.key: approval of a contract with the media center for cable casting services of public meetings for a term of three years and a not to exceed amo
log.title: Approval of a Contract with the Media Center for Cable Casting Services of Public Meetings for a Term of Three Years and a Not to Exceed Amount of $540,000 with Two One-Year Options to Extend for a Potential Not to Exceed amount of $900,000 over a five-year term, for cablecasting, production, and streaming services; CEQA Status – Not a Project.
log.draft: The City Council considers a three-year contract for cable and streaming services of public meetings.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline states 'three-year contract' but the official item specifies a three-year initial term PLUS 'Two One-Year Options to Extend.' This omits material contract structure that a resident needs to understand the full scope. The headline is incomplete rather than false, but it does not fully ground all facts from the source material.

log.title: SECOND READING: Ordinance of the Council of the City of Palo Alto Prohibiting Rental of Vehicles for Human Habitation (FIRST READING: December 8, 2025; PASSED 6-1, Lythcott-Haims no) Public Comment

log.key: study and assessment of palo alto turf systems and direct staff to proceed with el camino park synthetic turf replacement and natural grass
log.title: Study and Assessment of Palo Alto Turf Systems and Direct Staff to Proceed with El Camino Park Synthetic Turf Replacement and Natural Grass Pilot; CEQA Status – Categorically and Statutorily Exempt Presentation, Public Comment
log.draft: The City Council reviews turf systems and plans synthetic turf replacement at El Camino Park.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline states 'plans synthetic turf replacement' which goes beyond what the agenda item supports. The item directs staff to 'proceed with' replacement and includes a 'pilot' for natural grass—this is directional/instructional language, not a confirmed plan. The headline omits the natural grass pilot component, creating incomplete representation of the actual scope. Additionally, 'reviews turf systems' is somewhat vague compared to the specific 'Study and Assessment' framing in the official item.

log.key: public hearing / quasi-judicial. 2100-2400 geng road [24pln-00356]: recommendation on site and design to demolish four existing commercial b
log.title: PUBLIC HEARING / QUASI-JUDICIAL. 2100-2400 Geng Road [24PLN-00356]: Recommendation on Site and Design to Demolish Four Existing Commercial Buildings and Construct 65 Three-Story Buildings Containing 145 For Sale Townhome Units. Thirteen Percent (19) of the Units Would Be Deed Restricted to Low Income Households. The Project Also Includes a Conditional Use Permit for the Residential Use and a Vesting Tentative Map to Merge Two Parcels and Re-Subdivide For Condominium Purposes Which Council will Consider In Conjunction with the Site and Design Application. The Project is Proposed in Accordance with Builder’s Remedy (California Government Code Section 65589.5(d)(5)). A Senate Bill 330 Pre-Application was Filed on July 8, 2024. CEQA Status: Exempt from CEQA in Accordance with CEQA Guidelines Section 15183 (Comprehensive Plan Consistency). Zoning District: ROLM (E)(D)(AD) -Research, Office and Limited Manufacturing (Embarcadero) with Site and Design and Automobile Dealership Combining Districts.
log.draft: The Architectural Review Board reviews a proposal to demolish four buildings and construct 145 townhomes at 2100-2400 Geng Road.

log.title: PUBLIC HEARING / QUASI-JUDICIAL. 3781 El Camino Real [24PLN-00161]: Recommendation on a Major Architectural Review Application to Demolish Commercial and Residential Buildings Located at 3727-3737 & 3773-3783 El Camino Real, 378-400 Madeline Court, and 388 Curtner Avenue, and to Construct a Seven-Story, Multi-Family Residential Housing Development Project with 183 Units, 13 Percent of Which Would be Provided at a Rate Affordable to Low Income. log.verdict: checker rejected: The official agenda item specifies 'Endorsement of the Connect Bay Area Transit Initiative Public Comment' — indicating the item concerns public comment about a potential endorsement, not a direct consideration of endorsement itself. The candidate headline states 'considers endorsement,' which matches the official item's subject matter, but the official item text is not provided in full to verify that every fact in the headline is directly supported. Additionally, without the body text, grounding cannot be confirmed.
